source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/09_Usability/260303 사용목적 수정(CE 반영)/3. A-UEF-HNX_Usability Engineering File (Rev.2, 03032026).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering File for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB, Rev.2 dated March 03, 2026.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Defines the usability engineering process, use specification, hazard-related use scenarios, user interface requirements, usability verification, and summative evaluation approach for the Portable X-ray System.
keyPoints: The document states compliance with EN 62366-1:2015.
The variant model HnX-PB has only changed exterior design compared to HnX-P1, with interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software stated as the same.
The device is indicated as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy and is not to be used for mammography.
All usage scenarios identified in the hazard-related section were used for summative evaluation.
Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-08 are recorded as Pass with No issues.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: The H&abyz Co., Ltd. identifies known or foreseeable HAZARDS and HAZARDOUS SITUATIONS

entities.name: Usability Validation Plan
entities.evidence: Please refer to Usability Validation Plan (A-UVP-HNX) for details.

entities.name: Usability Validation Report
entities.evidence: The summative evaluation report is recorded in Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-UVR-HNX).

entities.name: Risk Management Report
entities.evidence: Possible hazards for each detailed task are integrated and managed in the Risk Management Report(A-RMR-HNX).

entities.name: Post-market Surveillance
entities.evidence: Please refer to the A-PMS-HNX Post-market Surveillance for further information on this.

concepts.name: Usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: The usability engineering process involves analyzing, designing, and verifying the user interface to ensure it meets usability and safety requirements.

concepts.name: Hazard-related use scenario
concepts.evidence: use scenario that could lead to a hazardous situation or harm

concepts.name: Summative evaluation
concepts.evidence: user interface evaluation conducted at the end of the user interface development with the intent to obtain objective evidence that the user interface can be used safely

concepts.name: Formative evaluation
concepts.evidence: user interface evaluation conducted with the intent to explore user interface design strengths, weaknesses, and unanticipated use errors

concepts.name: User interface of unknown provenance
concepts.evidence: UOUP may be evaluated according to Annex C of IEC 62366-1:2015. However, this clause is not applicable on our device, Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

concepts.name: Radiation exposure risk control
concepts.evidence: Justify all exposures based on clinical indication; Minimize number of exposures per patient; Use appropriate technique parameters per IFU; Avoid repeated exposures to same anatomical site; Follow radiation protection guidelines (ICRP 103, 135).

evidence.claim: The document is a Usability Engineering File for the Portable X-ray System models HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This document specifies the usability engineering process for the Portable X-ray System (Model Name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.2 dated March 03, 2026.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision History ... 2 ... March 03, 2026 ... Revision according to the intended purpose

evidence.claim: The document states compliance with EN 62366-1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose is to ensure safety by mitigating risks related to usability problems. This document complies with EN 62366-1:2015.

evidence.claim: HnX-PB is described as a variant model with only exterior design changed compared to HnX-P1, and no additional usability validation will be conducted for the variant addition.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model(HnX-PB) has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same. Therefore, no additional usability validation will be conducted for the addition of the variant model(HnX-PB).

evidence.claim: The intended use states the Portable X-ray System provides general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: The Portable X-ray System is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adults and children. It is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 and HnX-PB are battery-powered portable X-ray systems intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is a battery-powered portable X-ray system intended for generating radiographic images of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ③ Mammography: This device is not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device is contraindicated for abdominal and pelvic use in women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.
evidence.evidenceText: Pregnancy: The device is contraindicated for use in the abdominal and pelvic regions of women who are pregnant or may be pregnant.

evidence.claim: For bariatric patients, the device is strictly contraindicated for examinations other than extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: Bariatric Patients: The device is strictly contraindicated for any examination other than the extremities (i.e., limb imaging) in bariatric patients (defined as BMI 40 kg/m^2).

evidence.claim: The intended use environment is professional healthcare facilities indoors with radiation safety protocols and distance requirements maintained.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 (and HnX-PB) is intended for use in professional healthcare facilities, such as hospitals, clinics, and emergency departments. The device is limited to indoor operation within controlled environments where radiation safety protocols and distance requirements are maintained.

evidence.claim: The document lists safety-related functions including yellow irradiation lamp and buzzer during X-ray irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-rays are irradiated, the irradiation lamp lights up yellow and a buzzer sounds to alert you to the risk of X-ray exposure.

evidence.claim: The device stops irradiation immediately if the two-stage hand switch button is released during irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: If you release the 2-stage hand switch button during X-ray irradiation, the irradiation stops immediately.

evidence.claim: The document states no known hazards and hazardous situations associated with use errors were found for existing user interfaces of similar devices.
evidence.evidenceText: We investigated information about known hazards and hazardous situations in existing user interfaces for similar types of medical devices. However, there were no hazards and hazardous situations associated with use errors.

evidence.claim: Foreseeable use errors include dose setting mistakes causing overexposure to radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: Setting the exposure dose ... Mistakes in setting the exposure dose ... UR-06 ... Overexposure to radiation ... 3

evidence.claim: All identified usage scenarios were used for summative evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: All usage scenarios we identified in section 5.4 were used for summative evaluation.

evidence.claim: User interface verification results for UI-01 through UI-08 were Pass with No issues.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Verification Result ... UI-01 No issues Pass ... UI-08 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: User Interface of Unknown Provenance is not applicable to the Portable X-ray System HnX-P1 and HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: However, this clause is not applicable on our device, Portable X-ray System (HnX-P1, HnX-PB).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly stated in the source text; source metadata authoritySignal says FDA, but the document text cites EN, IEC, ISO, MDCG, and ICRP references and does not mention FDA.

Potentially conflicting intended population evidence: intended use says targeting both adults and children, target population says adult patients, and warning says the device is not intended for the pediatric population.
Prepared, reviewed, approved names and signatures appear in a table, but signature values are not present in the extracted text.
Some bilingual Korean-English table text and minor OCR artifacts are present, including duplicated wording and the manufacturer string 'H&abyz Co., Ltd.'.
